title: 2025 10 23 Petitions
displayTitle: 2025-10-23 - Petitions Committee - Petition of Catherine Cooke: Fund Keytruda for early stage triple negative breast cancer
topic: Petition of Catherine Cooke: Fund Keytruda for early stage triple negative breast cancer
description: Petition of Catherine Cooke: Fund Keytruda for early stage triple negative breast cancer Catherine Cooke Ah-Leen Rayner, CEO, Breast Cancer Foundation Mehdi Shahbazpour, Head of Research & Strategic Programmes, Breast Cancer Petition of Tony Gore: Explain MSD’s traffic light, sanctions, and generative AI use and proposals Tony Gore Cheryl Boyes Petition of Stiffany Adanza: Exclude Chronic Myeloid Leukaemia (CML) from Immigration Instructions A4.10.1 Stiffany Adanza Nicola Scotland, Principal Business Analyst, Immigration Health, Immigration NZ Polly Vowles, Policy Manager, Skills and Residence Immigration Policy, Ministry of Business, Innovation and Employment Dr Rob Kofoed, Chief Medical Officer, Immigration Health, Immigration NZ
descriptionSummary: Petition of Catherine Cooke: Fund Keytruda for early stage triple negative breast cancer. Catherine Cooke. Ah-Leen Rayner, CEO, Breast Cancer Foundation

'cause the New Zealand health system mandates saying I can only receive the unfunded drug in private.

And now all costs fall to me personally. I can only be treated in private again, the government is mandating. I can only get my unfunded drugs in private to live. Um, triple negative breast cancer is the most aggressive and deadliest form of breast cancer. It grows and spreads faster than other types of breast cancer and is most likely to come back. Currently. There is no funded treatment for early triple negative breast cancer apart from chemotherapy.

Keytruda is the only targeted treatment that works specifically for triple negative breast cancer. In October, 2024, after two years of advocacy, Keytruda was approved for funding by far neck, but only for the advanced stage of the disease where the cancer has spread and the disease has become terminal and not for the early stages of the breast cancer.

When we have an opportunity to reduce the risk of recurrence and go for a cure, this doesn't make sense. Medicines for advanced breast cancer are necessary, don't get me wrong. We need them because they prolong the life of patients who are, who have reached a advanced stage.

But we also need them for early stages of the disease because this is when we have the opportunity to reduce the risk of the cancer coming back on behalf of patients and clinicians in New Zealand as well as more than 17,000 people who have signed this petition.
